meta.title: Reversible Change in the Nucleoprotein Composition of Bromoviruses after Multiplication in Chenopodium hybridum L.

abstractHtml: Brome mosaic virus (BMV) and cowpea chlorotic mottle virus (CCMV), propagated in two varieties of Chenopodium hybridum L., showed a considerable change in the relative proportions of the RNA and nucleoprotein components, as compared to virus propagated in Hordeum vulgare and Vigna unguiculata respectively. The reversible change was independent of the type of infection in C. hybridum: local necrotic, local chlorotic or systemic.
